prompts: Cloning technology relies on genetic replication, primarily using somatic cell nuclear transfer (SCNT). In SCNT, the nucleus of a somatic cell, which contains the organism's DNA, is transferred into an enucleated egg cell, which has had its nucleus removed. This egg cell is then stimulated to divide and develop into an embryo, which is genetically identical to the donor of the somatic cell. This technique replicates the donor's DNA, enabling the production of a clone. Successful cloning requires precise manipulation of genetic material to ensure the resulting organism accurately reflects the genetic makeup of the original somatic cell source.
